description: Foundation types for unified discovery module (Engine, FeatureKind, Layout, DiscoveredFeature, DiscoveredSet, ScanCounts, SkipReason)
steps: Create directory crates/libaipm/src/discovery/ as a module folder (mod.rs sibling to today's discovery.rs which is left untouched in this step)

Add crates/libaipm/src/discovery/types.rs defining pub enum Engine { Claude, Copilot, Ai }, pub enum Layout { Canonical, CopilotSubroot, CopilotSubrootWithSkills, AiNested, AiPlugin }, pub struct DiscoveredFeature { kind, engine, layout, source_root, feature_dir, path } with derives Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq (renamed from feature.rs to types.rs to follow existing manifest/types.rs convention and avoid clippy::module_name_repetitions)
Move pub enum FeatureKind from today's discovery.rs into discovery/types.rs (re-export from the old location for now to avoid breaking lint callers)

Add crates/libaipm/src/discovery/scan_report.rs defining pub struct DiscoveredSet { features, scanned_dirs, skipped }, pub struct ScanCounts { skills, agents, hooks, instructions, plugins, marketplaces, plugin_jsons }, pub enum SkipReason { SkipDirByName, UnknownLayout, InvalidUtf8 } with Default impls
Implement DiscoveredSet::counts() that aggregates ScanCounts from features, and DiscoveredSet::is_empty()
Add crates/libaipm/src/discovery/mod.rs that pub uses Engine, FeatureKind, Layout, DiscoveredFeature, DiscoveredSet, ScanCounts, SkipReason

Wire the new module into crates/libaipm/src/lib.rs as `pub mod discovery;` (rename old discovery.rs to discovery_legacy.rs and declare as private mod discovery_legacy; existing imports still work via re-exports from discovery/mod.rs)
Add unit tests in types.rs and scan_report.rs covering Default impls, counts() aggregation across each FeatureKind, is_empty true/false, and SkipReason variant construction
Run cargo build --workspace, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check — all four must be green with zero warnings
Confirm no .unwrap()/.expect()/panic!()/println! anywhere per CLAUDE.md lint policy

description: Engine-root inference helper that walks ancestors to identify .claude/.github/.ai roots (replaces classify_source_context and source_type_from_path)
steps: Add crates/libaipm/src/discovery/source.rs with pub fn infer_engine_root(path: &Path, project_root: &Path) -> Option<(Engine, PathBuf)>
Walk path.ancestors() until project_root is reached; return on first ancestor whose file_name matches .claude (Engine::Claude), .github (Engine::Copilot), or .ai (Engine::Ai)
Handle the boundary case where path == project_root (return None)
Handle non-UTF-8 path components gracefully without unwrap (use to_string_lossy or skip)

Add unit tests covering: .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md → (Claude, .claude); .github/copilot/skills/<x>/SKILL.md → (Copilot, .github); .ai/<plugin>/skills/<x>/SKILL.md → (Ai, .ai); nested .ai/<plugin>/.claude/skills/<x>/SKILL.md → (Claude, the inner .claude); path with no engine ancestor → None
Re-export pub use source::infer_engine_root from discovery/mod.rs
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green

description: Skill layout grammar (THE customer fix for #725): any `skills` ancestor up to engine root, OR parent is a known engine sub-root (e.g. `copilot`)
steps: Add crates/libaipm/src/discovery/layout.rs with pub fn match_skill(path: &Path, engine: Engine, source_root: &Path) -> Option<DiscoveredFeature>
Implement the ancestor walk: collect file_name() of each ancestor between path's parent and source_root (exclusive)
Case A — any ancestor name == "skills" → return Some with FeatureKind::Skill and Layout chosen by pick_layout_for_skill()
Case B — engine == Engine::Copilot AND grandparent name == "copilot" → return Some with Layout::CopilotSubroot (the existing aipm accommodation)

Implement helper fn pick_layout_for_skill(ancestors, engine) that pattern-matches on the ancestors slice to pick: CopilotSubrootWithSkills (skills+copilot ancestors — the #725 case), CopilotSubroot (copilot only), AiNested (skills + dot-prefixed plugin ancestor), AiPlugin (Ai engine + skills), or Canonical (default)

Add unit tests covering ALL FIVE layouts plus issue #725's exact tree (.github/copilot/skills/skill-alpha/SKILL.md, skill-beta, skill-gamma) — assert each returns Some with the correct Layout variant and engine

Add negative tests: .github/copilot/SKILL.md (no skills ancestor, no <name> dir) → None; .github/SKILL.md → None; .github/skills/SKILL.md (flat) → still matched as Canonical per today's grandparent==skills branch
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green; coverage on layout.rs ≥89%

description: Layout matchers for non-skill features: agent, hook, plugin (aipm.toml), marketplace, plugin.json
steps: In crates/libaipm/src/discovery/layout.rs add pub fn match_agent(path, engine, source_root) — accepts <root>/agents/*.md per today's classify_feature_kind line 273-274
Add pub fn match_hook — accepts <root>/hooks.json AND <root>/hooks/hooks.json per today's classify_feature_kind line 275-276 plus today's CopilotHookDetector at copilot_hook_detector.rs:20-26
Add pub fn match_plugin — accepts <root>/<plugin>/aipm.toml when grandparent is .ai per today's classify_feature_kind line 277-278
Add pub fn match_marketplace — accepts .ai/<plugin>/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json per today's line 279-283
Add pub fn match_plugin_json — accepts .ai/<plugin>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json with great-grandparent == .ai per today's line 284-295
Each matcher returns Option<DiscoveredFeature> with the appropriate FeatureKind, Engine (from arg), Layout::Canonical for non-skill kinds, and feature_dir set to path.parent()
Unit tests for each matcher: positive cases (each canonical layout) and negative cases (wrong parent name, missing engine root)
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green

description: Instruction classifier with new `<engine>-instructions.md` regex (closes the `copilot-instructions.md` silent-drop gap)
steps: Add crates/libaipm/src/discovery/instruction.rs with const INSTRUCTION_FILENAMES: &[&str] = &["claude.md", "agents.md", "copilot.md", "instructions.md", "gemini.md"] (preserved from today's discovery.rs:186)

Implement <engine>-instructions.md detection via str::strip_suffix + matches! over the prefix set [copilot, claude, agents, gemini] — avoids adding the regex crate as a direct dep and dodges the .unwrap() concern on Lazy<Regex> static init
Add pub fn classify(file_name: &str, path: &Path, engine: Engine, source_root: &Path) -> Option<DiscoveredFeature>
Match logic: lowercase file_name, then return Some(Instructions) if (a) name in INSTRUCTION_FILENAMES OR (b) name ends with ".instructions.md" OR (c) matches_engine_instructions returns true

Add unit tests: copilot.md → match (case A); my-thing.instructions.md → match (case B); copilot-instructions.md → match (case C, the #725 fix); claude-instructions.md → match (case C); gemini-instructions.md → match; agents-instructions.md → match; reject instructions-copilot.md (wrong order); reject random.md; COPILOT-INSTRUCTIONS.md handled via case-insensitive lowercase
Wire pub use instruction::classify as classify_instruction from discovery/mod.rs
No regex dep added (str-only matching); zero .unwrap() in production code path
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green

description: Top-level classifier dispatch that routes a path to the right layout/instruction matcher
steps: Add crates/libaipm/src/discovery/classify.rs with pub fn classify(path: &Path, root: &Path, fs: &dyn Fs) -> Option<DiscoveredFeature>
Extract file_name and call source::infer_engine_root(path, root) — return None if no engine root found
Try instruction::classify first (filename-only, ancestry-independent) — if Some, return it

Match on file_name: SKILL.md → layout::match_skill; hooks.json → layout::match_hook; aipm.toml → layout::match_plugin; marketplace.json → layout::match_marketplace; plugin.json → layout::match_plugin_json; *.md (via path.extension() with eq_ignore_ascii_case to satisfy clippy::case_sensitive_file_extension_comparisons) → layout::match_agent; otherwise → None
Add unit tests covering the dispatch matrix: each filename × each engine combination, plus the #725 tree exactly
Re-export pub use classify::classify as classify_path from discovery/mod.rs
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green

description: Walker that wraps ignore::WalkBuilder with SKIP_DIRS, max_depth, source_filter, follow_symlinks=false
steps: Add crates/libaipm/src/discovery/walker.rs with pub fn walk(project_root: &Path, opts: &DiscoverOptions) -> Result<WalkResult, Error> where WalkResult carries Vec<PathBuf> of file paths and Vec<PathBuf> of directories scanned plus Vec<SkipReason>
Build ignore::WalkBuilder with hidden(false), git_ignore(true), git_global(true), git_exclude(true), follow_links(opts.follow_symlinks)
Apply opts.max_depth if Some via builder.max_depth(Some(d))
Add filter_entry that skips directories whose name is in SKIP_DIRS — emit a SkipReason::SkipDirByName for each via Arc<Mutex<Vec<SkipReason>>> shared between the closure and the post-walk drain
Defer source_filter to discover() (post-classification); walker walks the full tree per spec ('fail closed' alternative)
Collect Vec<PathBuf> of file paths and dirs walked; convert ignore::Error to Error::WalkFailed with .to_string(); sort outputs for determinism
Add pub struct DiscoverOptions { max_depth, source_filter, follow_symlinks } with #[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)] in discovery/mod.rs (anticipating feature 8)

Unit tests (11): empty dir, files+dirs, SKIP_DIRS pruning + recording, max_depth honored, dot-dirs visible, issue #725 tree visible, gitignored files excluded (with .git/ marker so the ignore crate honors .gitignore), follow_symlinks=false (Unix), skip-reason path recording, deep tree without max_depth, sorted output
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green; no .unwrap()/.expect() in production paths

description: Public discover() API with AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY feature flag and dispatch to walker + classifier
steps: DiscoverOptions { max_depth, source_filter, follow_symlinks } added in feature 7
Add pub fn discover(project_root: &Path, opts: &DiscoverOptions, fs: &dyn Fs) -> Result<DiscoveredSet, Error>
Read env var AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY via std::env::var; if value == "1" call unified_discover(...), else call legacy_discover(...)
unified_discover: call walker::walk, call classify::classify on each file, accumulate into DiscoveredSet { features, scanned_dirs, skipped }

legacy_discover (inline; feature 9 will extract to legacy_compat module): delegate to discover_features and map each legacy DiscoveredFeature → new types::DiscoveredFeature, dropping features whose path has no recognized engine ancestor
Apply opts.source_filter post-classification: retain only features whose source_root file_name matches
tracing::trace! on each classified feature, tracing::debug! on each skipped path
Add UNIFIED_DISCOVERY_ENV constant for the env-var name

Unit tests (8): unified_enabled return values, unified finds #725 tree (3 skills + 1 instructions), legacy finds canonical skills, legacy finds #725 skill files (today's grandparent==skills branch already does), legacy drops copilot-instructions.md while unified finds it (the actual lint-side #725 fix), unified carries walker skipped dirs, source_filter retains only matching, source_filter no-match returns empty, empty root, apply_source_filter no-filter keeps all. Tests serialize via static Mutex around env-var manipulation
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green

description: Legacy compat shim that wraps today's discover_features so the unified path is opt-in for one release
steps: Add crates/libaipm/src/discovery/legacy_compat.rs with pub fn discover_features_compat(root: &Path, opts: &DiscoverOptions, fs: &dyn Fs) -> Result<DiscoveredSet, Error>

Internally call today's discover_features(root, opts.max_depth) and adapt the Vec<DiscoveredFeature> output (today's type, which lives in discovery_legacy.rs) into the new DiscoveredSet wrapper with empty scanned_dirs and skipped (legacy didn't track these)

Adapt today's DiscoveredFeature shape (kind, source_context, path) into the new shape (kind, engine, layout, source_root, feature_dir, path) by inferring engine via source::infer_engine_root and setting layout = Layout::Canonical (legacy doesn't know layouts)
Update discovery::discover() to delegate the legacy branch to legacy_compat::discover_features_compat (replaces the inline legacy_discover from feature 8)

Unit tests (8): empty root, canonical skills round-trip with engine and layout assertions, #725 skill files visible via the legacy grandparent==skills branch, copilot-instructions.md dropped (the actual #725 lint-side bug, fix lives in unified path), project-root CLAUDE.md dropped (no engine ancestor — documented divergence), source_filter retains only matching, max_depth propagated to the legacy walker, .ai/ marketplace + plugin.json + aipm.toml all classified with engine=Ai
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green; coverage of legacy_compat ≥89%

description: Lint integration: replace lint's call to discover_features with discovery::discover; surface scan_counts and scanned_dirs in Outcome
steps: Switch crate::discovery::DiscoveredFeature re-export from legacy to new shape (types::DiscoveredFeature)
Edit crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs lint() to call crate::discovery::discover with DiscoverOptions { max_depth, source_filter: opts.source.clone(), follow_symlinks: false }

Update run_rules_for_feature to use new fields (path, engine, layout); is_inside_ai now checks Engine::Ai OR any .ai path component (preserves the legacy 'any .ai ancestor counts' semantics for nested .ai/<plugin>/.claude/ layouts)
Add scan_counts: ScanCounts and scanned_dirs: Vec<PathBuf> to Outcome; #[derive(Default)] on Outcome
Populate Outcome.scan_counts = discovered.counts() and Outcome.scanned_dirs = discovered.scanned_dirs
Remove inline --source post-filter (now handled inside discover())

Add classify::classify fallback for root-level instruction files: when infer_engine_root returns None, synthesize (Engine::Ai, project_root) so files like /repo/CLAUDE.md still classify as Instructions (preserves today's lint behavior on project-root instruction files)
Apply same fallback in legacy_compat::discover_features_compat to keep both paths consistent
Update lint::tests::lint_outcome_default_counts and 33 reporter.rs Outcome literals to include new fields via ..Outcome::default() spread
Update legacy_compat test 'project_root_instructions_dropped' to assert kept-with-Ai-fallback behavior (renamed to project_root_instructions_kept_with_ai_fallback)
Add discovery::test_env::with_unified_discovery_env helper module with shared static Mutex; both discovery::tests and lint::tests use it for env-var serialization across modules

Add 4 lint integration tests for the issue #725 fixture: unified mode finds 3 skills + 1 instructions; legacy mode finds 3 skills + 0 instructions (the silent drop being fixed); Outcome carries scan_counts and scanned_dirs; source_filter propagated to discover()
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green; coverage on lint/mod.rs preserved

description: Migrate Adapter trait + adapters::all() registry replacing the old Detector trait
steps: Add crates/libaipm/src/migrate/adapters/mod.rs with pub trait Adapter: Send + Sync with name(), applies_to(&DiscoveredFeature), to_artifact(&DiscoveredFeature, &dyn Fs) -> Result<Artifact, Error>
Add pub fn all() -> Vec<Box<dyn Adapter>> returning empty vec (12 adapters land in features 12 and 13)
Module-level doc-comment specifies first-match-wins resolution policy when multiple adapters claim the same DiscoveredFeature; orchestrator (feature 14) iterates all() in order
Keep the existing Detector trait at migrate/detector.rs ALIVE during the transition — feature 14's orchestrator switch is what removes it
Wire pub mod adapters into crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs
Unit tests (4): registry returns the right type, registry iterates safely when empty, stub adapter doesn't apply / has stable name, trait is dyn-object-safe (Box<dyn Adapter> compiles and dispatches)
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green

description: Migrate Copilot adapters (skill, agent, hook) — the FeatureKind-aligned subset
steps: Add crates/libaipm/src/migrate/adapters/skill.rs with CopilotSkillAdapter — reuses skill_common::parse_frontmatter, collect_files_recursive, extract_script_references on feat.feature_dir
Add adapters/agent.rs (CopilotAgentAdapter) — reuses skill_common::parse_frontmatter; strips .agent.md suffix when computing name from filename
Add adapters/hook.rs (CopilotHookAdapter) — reuses normalize_hook_events and extract_hook_script_references from copilot_hook_detector.rs (made pub(crate))
Modules are named by kind (skill/agent/hook), not engine, to dodge clippy::module_name_repetitions on CopilotSkillAdapter etc. Feature 13's Claude adapters will live in the same files
MCP / Extension / LSP / OutputStyle / Command adapters deferred to a follow-up feature — those kinds have no FeatureKind variants in the unified discovery today (see new feature item below)
Wire 3 Copilot adapters into adapters::all() in stable order: copilot-skill, copilot-agent, copilot-hook
Update legacy hook detector helpers to pub(crate) so the adapter can reuse them without code duplication
Per-adapter tests (5-7 each, 21 total): applies_to engine+kind gating, reject other engines/kinds, name stability, to_artifact roundtrip with a real fixture, error handling on bad inputs
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green

description: Migrate Claude adapters (skill, agent) — the FeatureKind-aligned subset
steps: Add ClaudeSkillAdapter to crates/libaipm/src/migrate/adapters/skill.rs alongside CopilotSkillAdapter — extracts only ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/ references (no ${SKILL_DIR}/ — Copilot-specific)
Add ClaudeAgentAdapter to crates/libaipm/src/migrate/adapters/agent.rs — uses ${CLAUDE_AGENT_DIR}/ prefix and records agent filename in `files`

ClaudeHookAdapter deferred: today's HookDetector reads .claude/settings.json which the unified discovery doesn't classify (no FeatureKind variant). Needs settings.json discovery support before the adapter can fire
ClaudeCommandAdapter / ClaudeMcpAdapter / ClaudeOutputStyleAdapter deferred: no FeatureKind variants for Command / Mcp / OutputStyle (same as Copilot deferred adapters)
Wire 2 Claude adapters into adapters::all() in stable order: copilot-skill, copilot-agent, copilot-hook, claude-skill, claude-agent

Per-adapter unit tests (7 each, 14 total): applies_to engine+kind, rejects-other-engine, rejects-other-kind, name stability, to_artifact roundtrip with real fixture, script-reference extraction with engine-specific prefix, error/edge cases
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green

description: Migrate orchestrator switch: replace migrate_recursive and migrate_single_source bodies with discovery::discover + adapters loop
steps: Edit crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs:387-479 (migrate_recursive) — replace `let discovered = crate::discovery::discover_source_dirs(dir, &[".claude", ".github"], max_depth)?;` and the per-source detector dispatch with `let discovered = crate::discovery::discover(dir, &opts.into(), fs)?;`

Replace the per-detector loop with: `for feat in &discovered.features { for adapter in adapters::all() { if adapter.applies_to(feat) { all_artifacts.push(adapter.to_artifact(feat, fs)?); break; } } }` (first-match-wins per the trait contract)
Edit crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs:301-384 (migrate_single_source) similarly — pass opts.source as DiscoverOptions::source_filter; remove the detectors_for_source fallback
Add From<&Options> for DiscoverOptions impl that maps max_depth, source, and follow_symlinks=false
Wire AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY check: when env var != "1" the function falls through to today's detector path (legacy_compat — keep for one release)
Update Outcome to include scan_counts and scanned_dirs (mirror to lint's Outcome change)
Update all migrate tests that construct Outcome to populate the new fields
Confirm reconciler.rs and emitter.rs are unchanged — they continue to consume Vec<Artifact> + plans the same way
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green; coverage on migrate/mod.rs preserved

description: Default scan-summary CLI output written to stderr by both cmd_lint and cmd_migrate (Goal G5)
steps: Add crates/aipm/src/scan_summary.rs with pub fn write_summary(out: &mut dyn Write, counts: ScanCounts, scanned_dirs: usize, sources: &[String]) -> std::io::Result<()>
Format: "Scanned {N} director{y|ies} in [{sources}]; matched {format_counts(counts)}\n" with format_counts producing "M skills, K agents, …" omitting zero categories or "0 features" if total == 0
Add a --quiet flag declaration to both Migrate and Lint subcommands in crates/aipm/src/main.rs:158-213 (use clap's Arg::action(ArgAction::SetTrue) on a bool field)
Wire write_summary into cmd_lint at crates/aipm/src/main.rs:708-788 BEFORE the reporter is invoked, gated on !opts.quiet, writing to std::io::stderr()
Wire write_summary into cmd_migrate at crates/aipm/src/main.rs:889-992 similarly, BEFORE the action-print loop
Add unit tests for format_counts: zero counts → "0 features"; one kind → "3 skills"; multiple kinds → "3 skills, 1 instructions" (no comma after last)
Add an E2E test invoking each command against the issue #725 fixture and asserting stderr contains "matched 3 skills, 1 instructions" and stdout has the per-action lines
Add an E2E test asserting --quiet suppresses the stderr summary
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green; verify use of write!()/writeln!() not println!()/eprintln!() per CLAUDE.md

description: BDD scenarios for issue #725 — Copilot CLI nested skills layout for both migrate and lint
steps: Edit tests/features/manifest/migrate.feature and add `Rule: Copilot CLI nested skills layout (.github/copilot/skills/) is discovered` immediately after the existing rule at line 187

Add Scenario: "Migrate skills from .github/copilot/skills/" with steps creating skill-alpha, skill-beta, skill-gamma per #725's tree, running `aipm migrate --source .github`, asserting all three skills are migrated and registered
Add Scenario: "Recursive migrate finds nested .github/copilot/skills/" running `aipm migrate` (no --source) and asserting the same outcome
Edit tests/features/guardrails/quality.feature to add a scenario: "Lint reports issues for skills under .github/copilot/skills/" with a SKILL.md missing description; assert lint exits non-zero
Edit tests/features/guardrails/quality.feature to add a scenario for `.github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md` triggering instructions_oversized when over budget

Add new BDD step `given_copilot_nested_skill_exists` in crates/libaipm/tests/bdd.rs that writes `<base>/.github/copilot/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` with given content (mirroring the existing given_copilot_skill_exists at line 756-766 but with the extra skills/ segment)
Add new BDD step `given_copilot_instructions_file_exists` writing `<base>/.github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md`
Run AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY=1 cargo test --workspace; all new BDD scenarios pass
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check

description: End-to-end integration tests against issue #725's exact tree for both commands
steps: Add crates/aipm/tests/issue_725_e2e.rs creating a tempdir with the exact tree from #725: .github/copilot/skills/{skill-alpha,skill-beta,skill-gamma}/SKILL.md plus .github/copilot/copilot-instructions.md plus a .ai/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json (so migrate has a valid output target)

Test 1 — `aipm migrate` (with AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY=1 in env): assert exit code 0, stderr contains "matched 3 skills, 1 instructions", stdout contains 3 "Migrated skill" lines, .ai/<plugin>/skills/{skill-alpha,…}/SKILL.md exist

Test 2 — `aipm lint` (with AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY=1): assert exit code 0, stderr contains the matched line, stdout's reporter output is sensible (human reporter shows "no issues found" for valid skills)
Test 3 — `aipm lint --quiet` against the same tree: assert stderr is silent

Test 4 — `aipm migrate` WITHOUT AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY (legacy path): assert exit code 0 AND zero migrated artifacts (today's bug behavior preserved when flag is off — this asserts the regression fence)
Test 5 — `aipm lint --reporter ci-github` with AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY=1 against the tree: assert stdout has zero diagnostics AND stderr has the scan summary
Use std::process::Command to invoke the compiled binary; do NOT use any test helper that bypasses the CLI
Run cargo build/test/clippy/fmt — all four green; new tests run in CI and locally

description: Documentation updates for the new CopilotSubrootWithSkills layout and CHANGELOG entry
steps: Edit docs/guides/migrate.md lines 60-67 to add `.github/copilot/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` to the table of recognized Copilot skill layouts
Edit docs/guides/migrating-existing-configs.md line 46 similarly

Add a CHANGELOG.md entry under the next unreleased version: `### Fixed - aipm migrate and aipm lint now detect skills under .github/copilot/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (issue #725)` and `### Fixed - aipm lint now recognizes <engine>-instructions.md files (e.g. copilot-instructions.md, claude-instructions.md)` and `### Added - aipm migrate and aipm lint print a scan summary by default; suppress with --quiet`
Add a CHANGELOG note about the AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY env var being default-off in this release
Verify the docs build (if there is a docs build step in CI) — confirm with `grep -l docs .github/workflows/`
No code changes; cargo build/test/clippy/fmt should still pass (no production code touched)

description: Coverage gate verification: 89% branch coverage workspace-wide; discovery/ module coverage ≥89% per-module
steps: Run the coverage pipeline from CLAUDE.md: `cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace`
Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch`
Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc`
Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs|lsp\.rs)'`
Verify TOTAL branch column ≥89%
Run `cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --html --open` and inspect each new file in crates/libaipm/src/discovery/ — every file ≥89% branch coverage
If any file is below the threshold, identify the uncovered branches and add focused unit tests to close them BEFORE marking this feature complete
Pay special attention to instruction.rs (the regex), layout.rs (the ancestor-walk pick_layout_for_skill match arms), and source.rs (the engine-root inference)

description: Phase 2 — flip AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY default to ON (separate release after Phase 1 soaks)
steps: Edit crates/libaipm/src/discovery/mod.rs to flip the default: when env var is not set, use unified path; only when env var == "0" use legacy_compat
Update CHANGELOG.md noting the default flip; mention how users can opt out with AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY=0 in case of issues
Update docs/guides/migrate.md and docs/guides/migrating-existing-configs.md to remove any "experimental" caveats
Run the full test suite and coverage gate again to confirm no regressions in the default-on configuration
This is a SEPARATE PR/release per spec §8.1 Phase 2 — do NOT bundle with Phase 1

description: Phase 3 — delete legacy_compat, the env-var check, and the FS-walking bodies of the old detectors (separate release after Phase 2)
steps: Delete crates/libaipm/src/discovery_legacy.rs (or the temporary discovery.rs that was renamed in feature 1)
Delete crates/libaipm/src/discovery/legacy_compat.rs
Remove the AIPM_UNIFIED_DISCOVERY env-var check from discovery::discover; the function unconditionally calls unified_discover
Delete crates/libaipm/src/migrate/detector.rs (Detector trait + claude_detectors + copilot_detectors + detectors_for_source)

For each old *_detector.rs (skill_detector.rs, agent_detector.rs, command_detector.rs, hook_detector.rs, mcp_detector.rs, output_style_detector.rs, copilot_skill_detector.rs, copilot_agent_detector.rs, copilot_hook_detector.rs, copilot_extension_detector.rs, copilot_lsp_detector.rs, copilot_mcp_detector.rs) — keep the parsing/validation helpers (skill_common etc.) but delete the Detector impl and the FS-walking bodies
Update mod declarations in crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs to drop the deleted modules
